title: Lichiardopol's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: For every $k \in \mathbb{N}$ there exists an integer $g(k) \in \mathbb{N}$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq g(k)$ contains $k$ vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths.
context_text: Lichiardopol [17] proved that every digraph with $\delta^+(D) \geq 4$ contains two vertex-disjoint directed cycles of distinct lengths, and conjectured this far-reaching qualitative generalization. The conjecture remains open even for $k = 3$; earlier work verified it for tournaments, regular digraphs, and digraphs of small order.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for strongly $s(k)$-connected digraphs for every $k$ (Theorem 3), and establishes the existence of an integer $K$ such that every digraph with $\delta^+(D), \delta^-(D) \geq K$ contains three vertex-disjoint directed cycles of pairwise distinct lengths (Theorem 5), making progress on the open case $k = 3$.
notes: Section 5, which the paper states contains a closing conjecture by the paper's own authors, is not included in the provided text and could not be extracted.
paper_title: Disjoint cycles with length constraints in digraphs of large connectivity or minimum degree
